title: Random expert accuracy audit (>=95% correct) + error fixes
context: A random expert audit is the medium-risk accuracy gate for the scaled dataset: a clinical-informatics/oncology reviewer judges a random sample for correctness, targeting >= 95% correct. Errors found are logged, fixed, and the correction is versioned. This protects against silently shipping wrong structured criteria.
objective: Run a random expert accuracy audit of the scaled dataset, document results, and fix and version any errors found, targeting >= 95% of the sample judged correct.
acceptanceCriteria: A random sample is reviewed by a clinical-informatics/oncology reviewer; >= 95% judged correct.
Errors are logged, fixed, and corrections are versioned in a correction log.
Audit method, sample size, and results are documented.
No individualized advice or eligibility determination is introduced; not-advice framing preserved.
